blocks.text: Barbara Blair, Heather Munoz, Kelly Snyder,

Status on the {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}} freedom-to-operate work as of today. The consolidated device description went across last week and I have written confirmation on the housing but not on the disposable element. Until the second piece is confirmed I am charting the housing only. That is not a delay, it is the order the work has to happen in.

The search strategy is approved and the first pass is run. The material set came back thinner than the technology warrants and I do not entirely trust it, so I am running a second pass against a classification the first did not reach before I hand anything to Kelly Snyder for the file histories. Assumption on the record: the cutoff is the one Heather Munoz set, and anything published after it is outside this analysis unless we are told otherwise in writing. Nothing in the set so far has changed my view of what needs charting, but I have not finished reading it and would not want that read as a conclusion.

Two asks, both for Barbara Blair. The released engineering drawings, please, not the marketing renderings; what we hold does not reconcile with the bill of materials, and I would rather ask twice than chart against a part Middleton, Sullivan and Wiley does not make. And send them electronically. We are all still working from home and nothing couriered to the office will reach me this month.
